description: Dashboard providing a general overview of the Camunda Platform performance

panels.description:
Measures the time from writing a user command to the append-only log until the processing engine reads and starts processing.

Combines all latency measurements in one panel to get a better overview.
panels.title: Latency overview (p50)

panels.description: Requests which hit the gateway and a response have been sent to the client per second.
panels.title: Input: Requests handled by Gateway per second on average

panels.description: Measures the percentage of dropped requests
panels.title: Backpressure dropping requests per second

panels.description: Shows general throughput of processing, writing, exporting and importing together
panels.title: Primary storage: throughput overview

panels.description: The current cluster load is defined by the current write rate divided by the write rate limit. The cluster load will display no data unless the flow control rate write limits are enabled.
panels.title: Cluster Load

panels.description: Element instance events that are processed per second by the processing engine
panels.title: Primary storage: Element instance events processed per second

panels.description: Measures the number of records that are not yet processed by the process engine (includes events, which will be skipped)
panels.title: Current processing backlog (queue size)

panels.description: Data (records/documents) written to the secondary storage, like elasticsearch.
panels.title: Output: Data written to secondary storage

panels.description: Measures the number of records that are not yet exported by the exporter (includes commands, which will be skipped)

targets.expr: max(max (zeebe_log_appender_last_committed_position{namespace=~"$namespace", partition=~"$partition"}) by(partition) - max (zeebe_exporter_last_exported_position{namespace=~"$namespace", partition=~"$partition"}) by(partition))

panels.title: Current exporting backlog (queue size)

panels.description:
The average percentage of CPU periods where the container ran but was throttled (stopped from running the whole CPU period).


container_cpu_cfs_periods_total -
Number of elapsed enforcement period intervals
container_cpu_cfs_throttled_periods_total -
Number of throttled period intervals	

https://github.com/google/cadvisor/blob/master/docs/storage/prometheus.md
https://stackoverflow.com/a/67316429/2165134
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UE7QX98-kO0
panels.title: CPU Throttling (AVG)

panels.description:
AVG CPU usage in a second per pod and container.

container_cpu_usage_seconds_total reports the container CPU usage time per second. How much of a second was used by the container on a certain CPU.

It can be over 1 second (as we might use several CPUs). Indicating how much of a CPU second (or in this sense how many CPUs) was/is used.

targets.expr: sum by (pod, container, service) (rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{container!~"", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"camunda.*"}[$__rate_interval]))

panels.title: Camunda CPU usage

panels.description:
AVG CPU usage in a second per pod and container.

container_cpu_usage_seconds_total reports the container CPU usage time per second. How much of a second was used by the container on a certain CPU.

It can be over 1 second (as we might use several CPUs). Indicating how much of a CPU second (or in this sense how many CPUs) was/is used.

targets.expr: max by (pod, container, service) (rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{container!~"", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"elastic.*"}[$__rate_interval]))

panels.title: Elasticsearch CPU usage

panels.description:
AVG CPU usage in a second per pod and container.

container_cpu_usage_seconds_total reports the container CPU usage time per second. How much of a second was used by the container on a certain CPU.

It can be over 1 second (as we might use several CPUs). Indicating how much of a CPU second (or in this sense how many CPUs) was/is used.
panels.title: Application CPU usage

panels.description: Memory limits and requests are taken from the k8 pod metrics.
panels.title: Process memory usage in %

panels.description:
Total amount of memory reserved for the JVM, where:

- Used means currently in use by live objects
- Committed means allocated to the JVM by the OS, and guaranteed to be available for usage
- Max means maximum possible committed memory, but not guaranteed
panels.title: JVM Memory usage

panels.description: Measures the number of write operations that are executed per second

targets.expr: sum by(pod, container, service) (rate(container_fs_writes_total{namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod", container!=""}[$__rate_interval]))

panels.title: Write IOPS per second on average

panels.description:
Measures the time from creating an instance until completion. The full process instance execution time.

Note: This is measured in the Metric exporter
Note: This is one of the strongest measures, including the complete round-trip, especially processing and exporting the backlog and the ES index fresh interval, etc.
panels.title: Process instance execution time

panels.description:
Measures the time from writing a user command to the append-only log until the processing engine reads and starts processing.

Note: When the processing backlog is high, this measurement will be high as well.
panels.title: Write-to-Processing latency (p50)

panels.description:
Measures the time from writing an event to the append-only log until the exporter reads and starts exporting it.

Note: When the processing and/or exporting backlog is high, this measurement will be high as well.
panels.title: Write-to-Exporting latency (p50)

panels.description:
Measures the time a record is staged in the Camunda Exporter buffer/bulk before flushing
Note: When the processing and/or exporting backlog is high, this measurement will be high as well.
panels.title: Camunda exporter flush latency

panels.description:
Measures the time from creating an instance in the client until the process instance is available via the REST API. Determining how long it takes until data is available for the user. 

Note: This is one of the strongest measures, including the complete round-trip, especially processing and exporting the backlog and the ES index fresh interval, etc
panels.title: Data available latency (p50)

panels.description:
Measures the time from creating an instance in the client until the process instance is available via the REST API. Determining how long it takes until data is available for the user. 

Note: This is one of the strongest measures, including the complete round-trip, especially processing and exporting the backlog and the ES index fresh interval, etc.
panels.title: Data availability latency (avg)

panels.description:
Measures the time from writing a user command to the append-only log until the processing engine reads and starts processing.

Note: When the processing backlog is high, this measurement will be high as well.
panels.title: Write-to-Processing latency

panels.description: Time taken to process a record
panels.title: Record Processing Duration

panels.description:
Measures the time from writing an event to the append-only log until the exporter reads and starts exporting it.

Note: When the processing and/or exporting backlog is high, this measurement will be high as well.
panels.title: Write-to-Exporting latency

panels.description: The AVG duration of exporting a record (in seconds)
panels.title: Exporting duration in seconds (avg)

panels.description: Measures the time a record is staged in the Elasticsearch exporter buffer/bulk before flushing
panels.title: Elasticsearch Exporter record buffer time

panels.description: The time it takes to flush the bulk request in the Elasticsearch exporter
panels.title: Elasticsearch Exporter (Flush Duration)

panels.description: Measures the time a record is staged in the Camunda Exporter buffer/bulk before flushing
panels.title: Camunda Exporter record buffer time

panels.description: The time it takes to flush the bulk request in the Camunda exporter
panels.title: Camunda Exporter (Flush Duration)

panels.description: Requests which hit the gateway and a response have been sent to the client per second.
panels.title: Requests handled by Gateway per second on average

panels.description: Shows general throughput of processing, writing, exporting and importing together
panels.title: Throughput overview

panels.description: Measures the average process instances that can be completed per second (root and sub-process instances).
panels.title: Completed Processes per second (PI/s) on average

panels.description: Measures the average number of archived PIs per second.
panels.title: Archived Processes per second (PI/s) on average

panels.description: Measures the average flow node instances that can be completed per second.
panels.title: Flow node instances per second (FNI/s) on average

panels.description: Measures the average number of service task instances that can be completed
panels.title: Service tasks per second (STI/s) on average

panels.description: Measures the average number of jobs that can be completed per second.
panels.title: Jobs per second (J/s) on average

panels.description: Measures the average number of records that can be processed per second. This is the most atomic metric we have for processing.
panels.title: Processed records per second on average

panels.description: Measures the average number of records that can be exported per second. This is the most atomic metric we have for exporting.
panels.title: Exported records per second on average

panels.description:
Measures the average number of indexed documents per second in Elasticsearch
Note: The records in Camunda != the Documents to be indexed in Elasticsearch, as data gets duplicated and some records get merged.
panels.title: Elasticsearch documents indexed per second on average

title: Camunda Performance
